title: selaginella cochleata
intro:
Selaginella cochleata, also known as the spoon-leaved selaginella or spoon-leaved spike moss, is a species of vascular plant in the genus Selaginella, which is a type of evergreen perennial that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Selaginella cochleata include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant, which can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds, with its antimicrobial properties helping to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection.
